channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IMF Raises Concern Over Unrecorded Government Spending Equivalent to 2% of Nigeria’s GDP
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) don raise serious concern about how Nigeria dey manage im public finances, especially as e relate to budget transparency and reporting of government spending. Acc...

item.title: Concerns arise after viral video shows damage to Trump arch replica at Washington, D.C. fair
item.description: A viral video circulating on social media has drawn attention to apparent structural issues affecting a scale replica of President Donald Trump’s proposed “Triumphal Arch,” displayed at the Great Amer...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif to visit Iran and Turkiye for condolence and bilateral talks on regional cooperation
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office has confirmed that Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif will undertake an official visit to Iran and Turkiye from July 3 to July 5. According to FO spokesperson Tahir Andrabi, the p...

item.title: Security operatives repel armed attack on NIPSS Kuru, one suspect killed
item.description: Security operatives have successfully repelled another armed attack on the National Institute for Policy and Strategic Studies (NIPSS) in Kuru, Plateau State, with one suspected attacker confirmed dea...

item.title: Cyclist takes legal action against Enfield Council after severe injuries caused by bike lane barrier
item.description: A cyclist from Edmonton, north London, is taking legal action against Enfield Council following a serious accident in which he sustained multiple injuries after colliding with a bike lane barrier. Gre...

item.title: Tanzania Reports Strong Revenue Growth in Mining, Dividends, Investment, and Approves TZS 433.38 Billion Livestock and Fisheries Budget for 2026/27
item.description: Tanzania has recorded strong fiscal and investment performance across several key economic sectors, showing improved revenue collection, rising dividends from state-owned enterprises, and increased in...

item.title: Clotted cream ice cream named best frozen dessert in global ranking
item.description: A global ranking of the top 50 frozen desserts has placed a traditional British favourite, clotted cream ice cream, in first position, ahead of internationally renowned options such as Italian gelato....

item.title: Concerns raised over UK–US pharmaceutical agreement and its potential impact on NHS funding and public health
item.description: The article is a critical opinion piece examining a reported pharmaceutical agreement between the UK government, led by Keir Starmer, and the United States. The deal is said to involve increased UK sp...

item.title: Police use familial DNA techniques to investigate suspected serial rapist linked to two attacks in south-east London
item.description: Metropolitan Police detectives are investigating a suspected serial rapist believed to be responsible for two separate attacks on women in south-east London, carried out four years apart. The first in...

item.title: Collapse of Ruto–Gideon Moi political truce amid dispute over The Standard newspaper coverage in Kenya
item.description: This article explains how a fragile political understanding between Kenya’s President William Ruto and Gideon Moi has broken down, less than a year before the country moves closer to the 2027 general ...

item.title: Anambra Police Commissioner Reiterates Need for Professional Conduct Among Officers
item.description: The Commissioner of Police in Anambra State, Ikioye Orutugu, has called on police officers in the state to maintain high standards of professionalism while carrying out their duties. He made this appe...

item.title: France’s attacking trio fuels debate over greatest World Cup forward line
item.description: France’s progress to the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup 2026 has been driven by an outstanding attacking unit that is now being compared to some of the best forward lines in football history. The combi...

item.title: Bhavin Turakhia invests $30M to develop AI-first enterprise workspace platform Neo
item.description: Indian entrepreneur Bhavin Turakhia is investing $30 million of his personal wealth into a new venture called Neo, an enterprise software platform designed to compete with productivity suites like Mic...

item.title: Letter questions Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu over Gaza conflict actions
item.description: This reader’s letter, published in Sowetan, offers a strongly critical view of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in relation to the ongoing conflict in Gaza. The writer reacts to reported rema...

item.title: Lahore court extends interim bail of PML-N MPA and his wife in harassment case involving actress Momina Iqbal
item.description: A sessions court in Lahore has extended the interim pre-arrest bail of Pakistan Muslim League-Nawaz (PML-N) Member of Provincial Assembly Saqib Chadhar and his wife until July 13 in a case related to ...

item.title: SFU researchers contribute to major CERN particle physics upgrade project
item.description: Researchers at Simon Fraser University (SFU) in Burnaby, British Columbia, are participating in a major international scientific collaboration centred on the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) at CERN, locat...

item.title: Wall Street Journal Warns Trump Family Financial Gains Could Create Political Risks for Republicans
item.description: A Wall Street Journal editorial board piece has raised concerns about Donald Trump’s financial activities and their potential political consequences for Republicans. According to the editorial, Trump ...

item.title: Municipalities face pressure as electricity tariff hikes strain affordability and sustainability
item.description: Municipalities across South Africa are facing increasing pressure as electricity tariff hikes place strain on both local government finances and residents’ ability to pay. From Wednesday, residents in...

item.title: Tanzania Highlights Job Creation and Local Participation in EACOP Project Implementation
item.description: The Tanzanian government has praised the progress and local involvement in the East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project, describing it as a major driver of employment, skills development, and p...

item.title: WA property developers take accounting firm to Supreme Court over alleged tax mismanagement
item.description: Two prominent Western Australian property entrepreneurs have initiated legal proceedings in the WA Supreme Court against their long-time accounting firm, HLB Mann Judd, alleging widespread professiona...
